question.text: Which of the following expenses qualifies as 'investment expenditure' rather than 'consumption expenditure'?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Investment expenditure creates an asset that generates future income. A rental property generates rental income and may appreciate in value. All other options are consumption expenditure — the money is spent without creating an income-generating asset. **The Trap:** Education expenses might seem like consumption but can be investment expenditure (increasing earning capacity). **Cross-Exam Context:** The consumption vs investment expenditure distinction is discussed in X-A (Ch 4). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Help clients allocate more income toward investment expenditure — every rupee spent on an income-generating asset works for you in the future.
